name: Sparkling Stardust
description: This tool provides CO2 concentration data for a user to compare recirculation cycles and satellite parking. There are only two inputs required: total number of students and students transported by car. There is an optional/adjustable input for distance to school used to calculate CO2 concentration at this distance. Gaussian dispersion is used to model the scenario because of a focus on steady-state vehicle emissions, a short range, and reasonably translating plume spread by vehicles. Four numerical outputs are created: total CO2 (kg/day), total vehicles per day, peak emission rate (g/s), and max concentration at a chosen distance; there is also a graphical representation of the CO2 concentration over distance curve.
